message:
feat(abi): eliminate glibc call-through for getopt, scheduler, PTY, ttyname, and nice with native implementations

Replaces glibc delegation with native/raw-syscall implementations for ~20 symbols:

- getopt/getopt_long: Full native POSIX option parser with short options,
  long option name matching, =value inline syntax, and -- termination
- nice: Raw SYS_getpriority/SYS_setpriority syscalls
- sched_* (7 functions): Direct libc::syscall(SYS_sched_*) wrappers
- grantpt/unlockpt/ptsname/posix_openpt: Raw ioctl/syscall PTY operations
- ttyname/ttyname_r: Native /proc/self/fd resolution with TIOCGWINSZ validation

All new implementations include runtime-policy membrane integration.
Adds 15 new tests covering happy paths, error paths, and kernel-syscall
parity checks. Updates support_matrix.json to reflect status upgrades
from GlibcCallThrough to Implemented/RawSyscall.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-      "status": "GlibcCallThrough",

-      "strict_semantics": "Delegates to glibc getopt for CLI option parsing.",
-      "hardened_semantics": "Same delegation.",
-      "default_stub": "Returns -1"
+      "strict_semantics": "Native short-option parser honoring optarg/optind/opterr/optopt global state and POSIX '?' / ':' signaling",

+      "default_stub": false

+      "strict_semantics": "Native long-option parser supporting no/required/optional args plus fallback to short-option parsing",
